POLARIS MISSION (this wheel): Make the agentic outline top-benchmark: it must COMPUTE-and-PROVE numbers (the moat frontier products lack), process the FULL 346-basket corpus with cp4_used=agentic (never fallback-plain; seed-fallback only on GLM-5.2 reasoning truncation), and build the rich toolkit + hard battery from the redesign doc so the downstream report scores top on RACE/DeepTRACE. Read /workspace/POLARIS/docs/agentic_outline_redesign.md (read-only) for the binding T1/T2 spec.
SUCCESS IS MEASURED BY: (a) A computed number (e.g. a delta/ratio) can be COMPUTED by the agent AND RENDER in the outline via the verified [#calc:] lane (quantified_analysis.py:617), never via [CITE:ev_xxx] — proven end-to-end on one real case; (b) the T1 toolkit tools land and pass real unit tests; (c) the T2 battery runs and S0/S1 failures trend to zero. Faithfulness invariant is the hard gate: NO derived/unverified number may ever reach the [CITE:ev_xxx] render path (that would be a P0 breach).
The bar is not "bug fixed" — it is the benchmark number at the top, VERIFIED. Keep going until the
measurement says top (or you hit a hard external blocker you must report honestly).

FENCE (military discipline — no exceptions):
- Work ONLY inside /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent (branch bot/outline-agent-box). NEVER touch another worktree.
- NEVER edit anything under /home/polaris/polaris_project (operator's monitoring dir — deck.sh, wheel*.js, wheels.py).
  If you catch yourself editing a monitoring script or a workflow .js, you have DRIFTED — stop.
- Report REAL output only. Never invent, never claim a pass you did not observe, never fake a benchmark number.
- Commit every landed step in /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent; append one dated line to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/WHEEL_PROGRESS.md.


YOU ARE OPUS — the hands AND the eyes. Do the hard work yourself: read the exact files named, search
where needed, build, fix, run, MEASURE. Do not wait for anyone to read the code for you.

CURRENT STATE (accurate as of wheel start — verify, don't assume):
worktree /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent @ HEAD 98726ca (branch bot/outline-agent-box). Inherited work is SOLID and PASSES: 19 tests green in ~2.8s. execute_python is un-bricked. The compute-reachability P1 is HALF-CLOSED: the SURFACING half is DONE — summary_for_llm(include_results=True) emits a computed-stats digest at analysis_notebook.py:227-276. The RENDER half is STILL OPEN at outline_agent.py:1609 — a computed number cannot yet render through the verified [#calc:] lane. NOT BUILT YET: src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_toolkit.py does not exist; all 9 non-compute T1 tools are missing; scripts/outline_battery.py does not exist; the fold-in seam (_offset_renumber outline_agent.py:610 + _stamp_and_delete :792) is NOT yet factored as a reusable unit.

YOUR ACTIONS THIS ROUND (precise; do these, in order, then MEASURE):
STEP 1 (close the render-half P1, highest correctness value): trace outline_agent.py:1609 and the [#calc:] lane at quantified_analysis.py:617; make a computed number render through [#calc:] end-to-end on ONE real case, and PROVE with a test that a derived number routed to [CITE:ev_xxx] is still DROPPED by strict_verify check (d). NOTE: a prior experiment already proved the [#calc:] render works — it rendered the Adobe FY2015->FY2016 operating income delta $590,000,000.00 via [#calc:opinc_delta...] with CALC LANE kept=1 dropped=0. Now HARDEN that into committed code + a real test, and confirm the [CITE:ev_xxx] drop guard. Commit. STEP 2 (factor the fold-in seam): extract _offset_renumber(:610)+_stamp_and_delete(:792) into a shared reusable unit with an id-collision test — unblocks fetch_url. Commit. STEP 3 (build T1 outline_toolkit.py): register_outline_toolkit() wiring the 9 tools + verified_compute per redesign doc PART 4; per-tool unit tests, commit incrementally. Do STEP 1 first.

RULES:
- Actually EXERCISE every change (run it; a heartbeat/concurrency probe, a real test, a real score —
  py_compile is NOT proof). Commit what passes.
- MEASURE the success metric ((a) A computed number (e.g. a delta/ratio) can be COMPUTED by the agent AND RENDER in the outline via the verified [#calc:] lane (quantified_analysis.py:617), never via [CITE:ev_xxx] — proven end-to-end on one real case; (b) the T1 toolkit tools land and pass real unit tests; (c) the T2 battery runs and S0/S1 failures trend to zero. Faithfulness invariant is the hard gate: NO derived/unverified number may ever reach the [CITE:ev_xxx] render path (that would be a P0 breach).) with a real number this round if at all possible.
- End by writing ONE precise question for Fable in "needs_fable" — a decision or a
  correctness/faithfulness/gap-to-top judgment. Do NOT ask Fable to read or search the codebase; you
  already did that. Give Fable the diff summary + the measurement, not a reading assignment.

10	  No tool result renders directly; external content re-enters through the fold-in seam

19	
20	## PART 1 — THE RICH TOOLKIT
21	
22	Target: ~34 first-party tools across 8 categories, plus an MCP adapter layer that grows the
23	registry to hundreds with ZERO driver changes. Everything speaks the existing contract:
24	`ToolDefinition` / `ToolResult(source_evidence_ids=...)` (tools/tool_registry.py) — the driver
25	(`OutlineAgent`, outline/outline_agent.py:990) does not change shape when tools are added.
26	
27	Legend: [E] = exists today (11 total). [W] = existing dormant module, wrap as a tool (~15-60
28	LOC each). [N] = genuinely new code.
29	
30	### Category A — Retrieval & re-retrieval (get more real evidence)
31	1.  search_more_evidence [E] — full pipe: query-derive -> run_live_retrieval -> S2 stamp -> fold-in (outline_agent.py:638).
32	2.  fetch_url [N] — targeted re-fetch of ONE named URL (agent saw a citation/link inside evidence
33	    and wants the primary). Reuses the run_live_retrieval fetch stack in single-URL mode
34	    (Zyte/crawl4ai fallback chain), then the SAME `_offset_renumber` + `_stamp_and_delete`
35	    fold-in seam as search_more_evidence. This is the tool that turns "source B cites source A"
36	    into actually reading source A.
37	3.  search_scholar [W] — scholarly search via existing tools/openalex_client.py + SemanticScholar
38	    backend already inside live_retriever; returns candidate works (title/venue/year/DOI/abstract)
39	    WITHOUT folding in; agent then fetch_url's the ones it wants. Cheap look-before-fetch.
40	4.  search_corpus [N] — BM25/keyword search over the CURRENT ev_store (rank/score + snippet per
41	    hit). Read-only, no network. The "do we already have this?" tool — mandatory first stop
42	    before any live fetch (the decide prompt says so; the battery asserts it on the saturated
43	    negative-control case).
44	5.  get_evidence [N] — return the FULL text + metadata of one evidence row by ev_id (the agent
45	    currently only sees the digest). Read-only. ~15 LOC.
46	6.  inspect_basket [E] — basket members, corroboration, assignment status (outline_agent.py:863).
47	7.  list_baskets [N] — one-line-per-basket index (id, claim gist, member count, weight range,
48	    assigned section) so the agent can scan the whole corpus shape without 300 inspect calls.
49	
50	### Category B — Document / structure parsing (read what a page really contains)
51	8.  parse_table [W] — extract tables from an evidence row (HTML `<table>` parse + PDF tables via
52	    existing tools/pdf_table_extractor.py) into typed rows {value, unit, row/col label,
53	    ev_id, char-span}. Output cells carry spans so they are verified-compute-eligible.
54	9.  read_spreadsheet [N] — open a fetched .xlsx/.csv artifact (openpyxl/csv), expose
55	    sheet/range/filter reads as typed rows with file+cell provenance. GAIA-class capability.
56	10. parse_figure [N] — chart/figure data extraction from an image or PDF figure via a
57	    vision-capable open-weight model (qwen-VL family per sovereignty; env-locked model id).
58	    HONESTY CONTRACT: output values are tagged `figure_estimated=True` and are NEVER
59	    verified-compute-eligible (no numeric-verbatim span exists); they may guide outline
60	    structure and trigger re-retrieval for the underlying data table, and render only as
61	    labeled approximations. Fail-open to "figure unreadable, disclosed".
62	11. extract_numeric_data [E] — regex/heuristic numeric extraction (tool_registry.py).
63	12. extract_entities [N] — typed entity extraction over selected rows (dates, orgs, tickers,
64	    drugs/dosages, genes, standards, statutes) via the mirror model, each entity pinned to
65	    ev_id+span. Feeds kg_ops and find_contradictions.
66	13. parse_pdf_layout [W] — mineru-based full-layout PDF parse (already deployed for fetch);
67	    exposed as an on-demand tool for a SPECIFIC document the agent cares about (sections,
68	    headings, references list).
69	
70	### Category C — Compute & statistics (derive numbers — two lanes, never confused)
71	14. execute_python [E] — LLM-written sandboxed script (code_executor). EXPLORATORY lane:
72	    agent-internal, BARRED from rendering. Unchanged.
73	15. calculator [N] — single whitelisted-AST arithmetic expression (reuses tradeoff_modeler's
74	    `_eval_formula` machinery, :419). Deterministic, no LLM, no sandbox spin-up, sub-second.
75	    For the 80% of compute needs that are one subtraction/ratio.
76	16. verified_compute [W] — THE moat tool. Wraps synthesis/tradeoff_modeler.py
77	    `build_quantified_spec` (:782) + `render_script` (:1196) + code_executor execution +
78	    Regime-C replay: agent supplies {question, candidate sourced inputs (ev_id + raw_literal),
79	    formula intent}; the tool validates fail-closed (literal_span_is_faithful :68, AST
80	    whitelist, material dependency) and returns a ComputedClaim that is the ONLY compute
81	    output allowed to render. Currently wired only into old graph_v3 — this registration is
82	    the W3 wire.
83	17. statistical_summary [E] · 18. comparison_table [E] · 19. meta_analysis [E] ·
84	    20. rank_by_impact [E] — unchanged (tool_registry.py).
85	21. time_series_math [N] — deterministic ops over DATED datapoints: delta, %change, CAGR,
86	    moving average, linear trend, YoY alignment. Pure functions over parse_table /
87	    extract_numeric_data outputs; results carry input ev_ids and are verified-compute-eligible
88	    when every input cell has a span (routes through the ModelSpec lane for rendering).
89	22. unit_convert [N] — new tools/unit_converter.py: pint-based unit conversion (mass, volume,
90	    energy, concentration incl. mg/dL<->mmol/L with analyte molar masses table), date
91	    arithmetic (fiscal-year alignment, ISO parsing), currency conversion with a DATED FX table
92	    (ECB reference rates, fetched once + cached as evidence rows so the rate itself is cited).
93	    Deterministic; conversion factor + source disclosed in the result.
94	23. sql_over_tables [N] — load parse_table/read_spreadsheet outputs into the existing
95	    EvidenceDatabase SQLite (tools/evidence_database.py) as ad-hoc tables and query them
96	    (joins, group-bys, conditional sums). Extends query_evidence_sql [E, 24] which stays for
97	    the evidence-metadata table.
98	
99	### Category D — Cross-source reasoning (agreement, conflict, identity)
100	25. agreement_analysis [E] — pairwise Jaccard consensus (tool_registry.py). Kept as the cheap
101	    screen only.
102	26. find_contradictions [N] — the real one: for a claim/aspect, pull the basket's members,
103	    run pairwise NLI-entailment (mirror model, same engine family the verifier uses) +
104	    numeric_comparator on extracted values; returns {agree, conflict, direction-of-conflict}
105	    pairs with ev_ids. Conflicts become outline material ("conflicting evidence" sub-theme),
106	    NEVER silently averaged.
107	27. corroboration_profile [N] — per-basket: distinct works, tier/weight spread,
108	    independent-vs-derivative sources (same-DOI / same-publisher collapse). The "is this
109	    claim really multi-source?" tool.
110	28. citation_lookup [W] — DOI/Crossref/OpenAlex metadata for a work: venue, year, citation
111	    count, OA status, and RETRACTION status (Crossref retraction watch field). Wraps
112	    openalex_client. Retracted -> weight-demote + disclose (never delete: §-1.3, it is
113	    on-topic credible-history content; the retraction itself is evidence).
114	29. kg_ops [N] — lightweight knowledge-graph over extract_entities output held in the
115	    workspace (networkx, in-memory): neighbors(entity), paths(a,b), co-mention clusters.
116	    Structure discovery for outline sub-themes ("these 12 baskets share entity X").
117	    Phase-2 of the build (not needed for battery v1).
118	
119	### Category E — Outline operations (the agent's own artifact)
120	30. update_outline [E] — validated revision ops via outline_revise (outline_agent.py:902).
121	31. coverage_audit [N] — deterministic accounting (never-LLM): unassigned basket ids,
122	    per-section basket counts, residual fraction, sections below floor — the O2/O3 math as an
123	    on-demand tool so the agent can SEE its own coverage instead of waiting for the checklist.
124	    Wraps the section_basket_map stats code.
125	32. preview_section_evidence [N] — for one section: its assigned baskets' claim gists +
126	    weights, exactly what compose will receive. The "walk in the reader's shoes" tool.
127	33. finish_outline [E] — terminal action (driver bounces via checklist; not self-certifying).
128	
129	### Category F — MCP extensibility layer (how 34 becomes hundreds without a rewrite)
130	34. tools/mcp_tool_adapter.py [N] — `McpToolBridge`:
131	    - Config: config/settings/outline_mcp_servers.yaml — list of servers {name, transport
132	      (stdio|http), command/url, allow-list of tool names, content_policy}. LAW VI: file +
133	      env override, no hardcoding. Seat: PG_OUTLINE_MCP (default OFF).
134	    - On startup: open sessions (official `mcp` python SDK), `tools/list` each server,
135	      register every allowed tool as `ToolDefinition(name=f"mcp__{server}__{tool}",
136	      description=<server-provided>, execute=<bridge call>)`. The driver needs ZERO changes —
137	      it already iterates the registry.
138	    - CONTENT POLICY (the faithfulness seam, non-negotiable): every MCP result is classified
139	      by the server's declared `content_policy`:
140	      (a) `external_content` (default) — result text becomes candidate evidence rows and MUST
141	          go through the standard fold-in seam (offset-renumber + URL-dedup + S2 stamp
142	          chrome-delete/topic-judge) before the agent can cite it. It NEVER goes straight
143	          into outline text or ToolResult.markdown as fact.
144	      (b) `pure_compute` — result is a derived value over already-folded evidence; it stays in
145	          the exploratory lane (barred from rendering) unless re-derived through
146	          verified_compute.
147	      This one rule is what lets the toolkit grow to hundreds of tools while the faithfulness
148	      engine remains the only hard gate.
149	    - §8.4: MCP server subprocesses are tracked in state/active_processes.json and torn down
150	      at agent exit.
151	
152	### Scaling the decide step past 20 tools
153	The current `_decide` prompt (outline_agent.py:1152) lists every tool. That holds to ~60 tools
154	(one-liners, ~2k tokens, trivial at glm-5.2's 1M ctx). Changes now:
155	- Registry gains `tags: list[str]` on ToolDefinition and `core: bool`. Decide prompt = CORE
156	  set in full (search_more_evidence, fetch_url, search_corpus, get_evidence, inspect_basket,
157	  update_outline, coverage_audit, calculator, verified_compute, finish_outline) + a grouped
158	  one-line INDEX of everything else by category.
159	- Add meta-tool `list_tools(category|keyword)` returning full descriptions on demand (mirrors
160	  how Claude Code defers 360+ tools behind ToolSearch). When MCP pushes the count past ~60,
161	  the index collapses to category headers + list_tools becomes the discovery path. No driver
162	  rewrite at any size.
163	
164	### New-vs-existing tally
165	Existing kept: 11. Wraps of dormant modules: 6 (search_scholar, parse_table, parse_pdf_layout,
166	verified_compute, citation_lookup, + query_evidence_sql extension). New first-party: 17.
167	Total first-party ≈ 34. MCP layer: unbounded.

171	## PART 2 — THE HARD EDGE-CASE BATTERY (22 cases)
172	
173	Design rules: every case is (i) REAL data (fixtures = really-fetched rows stored under
174	tests/battery/fixtures/ — LAW II compliant; live cases fetch real primary sources), (ii)
175	answer-verifiable (published gold number) OR behavior-verifiable (a deterministic assertion on
176	the transcript/ledger, never on prose wording), (iii) tagged with the capability it stresses.
177	Benchmark provenance: FinanceBench arXiv:2311.11944, GAIA arXiv:2311.12983, DiscoveryBench
178	arXiv:2407.01725, HLE-style synthesis, FS-Researcher checklist eval (arXiv:2602.01566),
179	DeepTRACE-style adversarial probes.
180	
181	FINANCE
182	- H01 [FinanceBench, gold] Adobe operating income change FY2015->FY2016. Stresses: targeted
183	  fetch of 2 real 10-Ks + parse_table + verified_compute subtract. Expected: gold delta,
184	  trace shows fetch->extract(span-pinned)->compute; a hand-waved number with no compute step
185	  = FAIL even if numerically right.
186	- H02 [FinanceBench, gold] Activision 3-yr (FY2017-19) average capex/revenue. Stresses:
187	  multi-document multi-hop (6 extractions, 3 ratios, 1 mean) inside one verified ModelSpec.
188	  Expected: gold ratio; all 6 inputs literal_span_is_faithful.
189	- H03 [new, gold-derivable] Total shareholder return across a stock split (e.g. NVDA 2021
190	  4-for-1): raw price series naive delta vs split-adjusted. Stresses: domain-trap awareness +
191	  time_series_math. Expected: split detected (it is in the fetched filings/press), adjusted
192	  return computed, adjustment disclosed. Naive unadjusted number rendered as the answer = FAIL.
193	- H04 [new, gold-derivable] Compare FY revenue of a USD-reporting vs EUR-reporting company.
194	  Stresses: unit_convert with DATED FX + fiscal-year alignment (different FY ends). Expected:
195	  conversion at a disclosed dated rate, FY misalignment disclosed; mixing currencies raw = FAIL.
196	- H05 [GAIA L1 adaptation, gold 89706.00] Conditional SUM of food (not drink) sales from a
197	  real xlsx. Stresses: read_spreadsheet + sql_over_tables filtered aggregate. Expected: exact
198	  gold; trace shows the filter predicate.
199	
200	SCIENCE / MEDICAL
201	- H06 [operator's case, gold via SEER] US female breast-cancer age-adjusted incidence change
202	  2000->2019, absolute + percent. Stresses: authoritative-source retrieval (SEER/CDC WONDER)
203	  + verified_compute. Expected: matches re-run of the SEER query; cites SEER not a news
204	  rewrite as primary.
205	- H07 [contradiction, behavior] Corpus seeded with two real RCTs reporting OPPOSITE effect

291	Purpose: run the whole battery AT ONCE, get a ranked failure list in minutes, fix, re-run only

294	### Layout
295	- tests/battery/cases/h01_adobe_opinc.yaml ... h22_poison_outlier.yaml — one spec per case:
296	  {id, domain, question, corpus: {fixture: <path> | live: <recipe>}, env_overrides,

298	  {type: trace_requires, steps: [...]}, {type: retrieval_count, op, n},
299	  {type: ledger_state, ...}, {type: disclosure_required, key}], holdout: false, variants: k}
300	- tests/battery/fixtures/<case>/ — REAL fetched rows (cp2-format JSON), stored once.
301	- scripts/outline_battery.py — the runner (new, ~400 LOC).
302	- outputs/battery/<run_id>/<case_id>/ — per-case sandbox: ev_store copy, checkpoints,
303	  transcript.jsonl, result.json, cost.jsonl, log.
304	- outputs/battery/<run_id>/ranked_failures.md + summary.json — the wheel's output.
305	
306	### Concurrency & isolation
307	- ProcessPoolExecutor, max_workers = PG_BATTERY_CONCURRENCY (default 6; the box is an A100
308	  host with ample CPU — LLM calls are network-bound so 6-12 cases in flight is cheap locally).
309	  Each case = ONE subprocess: own env copy (env_overrides applied to the child env dict, the
310	  parent's os.environ is NEVER mutated), own output dir, own EvidenceDatabase (in-memory /
311	  per-dir sqlite), own checkpoint namespace (workspace out_dir passed per case), own log file.
312	  Zero shared writable state between cases.
313	- Live-retrieval cap: a CROSS-PROCESS counting semaphore (fcntl flock slots file
314	  outputs/battery/.retrieval_slots, PG_BATTERY_LIVE_FETCH_SLOTS default 3) wraps
315	  run_live_retrieval entry inside battery mode — respects API rate limits and the container
316	  PID cap (fetch concurrency already 16). Fixture-corpus cases (17 of 22) do zero live fetch
317	  and parallelize freely; only H01/H02/H06/H18 + live variants contend for slots.
318	- OpenRouter: per-case client instances, per-case cost ledger; battery aborts a case (not the
319	  run) at PG_BATTERY_COST_CAP_PER_CASE.
320	- Timeout: per-case hard kill at budget wall + 120s grace (the process is killed, result =
321	  TIMEOUT, severity S3) — one wedged case can never stall the wheel.
322	
323	### Result collection & failure ranking
324	Each case emits result.json: {case_id, variant, assertions: [{name, pass, expected, actual}],
325	final_answer, turns, retrieval_calls, wall_s, cost, transcript_path}. The collector ranks by
326	severity:
327	- S0 FAITHFULNESS BREACH — a wrong/unsupported number rendered as VERIFIED; injection obeyed;
328	  id-collision assert tripped; retracted source used as sole support silently. Blocks
329	  everything; fix first.
330	- S1 WRONG ANSWER, UNDISCLOSED — gold missed and no UNFILLED/uncertainty disclosure.
331	- S2 CAPABILITY MISS, HONEST — gold missed but correctly disclosed as unfilled/approximate
332	  (the agent knew its limits). These are the capability roadmap, not blockers.
333	- S3 BEHAVIOR — redundant retrieval on saturated, budget/wall overshoot, degenerate looping,
334	  timeout.
335	- S4 cosmetic.
336	ranked_failures.md lists S0 first, each with: the failed assertion diff, the exact transcript
337	line range, and the suspected component (tool name from the trace). Ties break by frequency
338	across paraphrase variants (a case failing on all 3 variants outranks a 1-of-3 flake).
339	
340	### The fast test->fix->retest loop (wheel cadence)
341	1. `python scripts/outline_battery.py --all` — full battery, 6-way. Expected wall: <30 min
342	   (each case ≤600s, mostly parallel).
343	2. Read ranked_failures.md top-down. Fix the top S0/S1 cluster (Fable diagnoses root cause,
344	   Opus builds, per the standing workflow).
345	3. `--only-failed --baseline <run_id>` — re-runs ONLY previously-failing cases (+their
346	   variants). Minutes, not hours.
347	4. Repeat 2-3 until zero S0/S1 on the non-holdout set.
348	5. LOCK GATE: full `--all --include-holdout` clean of S0/S1 + a FRESH judge (never the
349	   builder — §-1.1, independent line-audit rule) reads 5 sampled transcripts line-by-line at
350	   the context level and confirms the PASSes are real. Only then is the wheel iteration
351	   signed off.
352	Every run appends one line to outputs/battery/history.jsonl (run_id, git sha, pass counts by
353	severity) — the convergence record the operator can hear read out plainly.
354	
355	### Anti-overfit (how it stays general)
356	- HOLDOUT: ~25% of cases (H04, H13, H15, H17, H22 initially) are excluded from all fix
357	  iterations; run ONLY at lock gates. A fix that passes iteration cases but fails holdout is
358	  overfit by definition — root-cause again.
359	- PARAPHRASE VARIANTS: each behavioral case carries k=3 auto-generated question rewrites
360	  (generated once, frozen in the yaml); assertions are variant-invariant. Kills
361	  prompt-string-matching fixes.
362	- ASSERTIONS ON BEHAVIOR AND GOLD ONLY — never on tool-call ORDER, never on prose wording,
363	  never on word/citation counts (§-1.1). Multiple valid tool paths to the same gold all pass.
364	- NO CASE-ID LEAKAGE: CI grep gate — battery case ids and gold literals must not appear
365	  anywhere under src/ (a fix referencing h01/89706.00 in production code fails the gate).
366	- DOMAIN BALANCE: the ranked report also groups failures by domain; a fix wave is not
367	  accepted if it improves one domain while regressing another (history.jsonl makes this
368	  visible).
369	- Rotation: each lock adds 2-3 NEW cases sourced from fresh benchmark items before the next
370	  wheel iteration, so the battery is a moving target.

802	async def _stamp_and_delete(
803	    rows: list[dict[str, Any]], research_question: str, agent_model: str,
804	) -> tuple[list[dict[str, Any]], list[dict[str, Any]], list[dict[str, Any]]]:
805	    """S2 stamp pass: content-integrity chrome detection (pure, no LLM) THEN semantic
806	    topic-judge off-topic screen (LLM, fail-open). Returns (kept, deleted_chrome,
807	    deleted_offtopic). §-1.3.1: only genuine junk/off-topic is deleted here — the rest of
808	    the WEIGHT-AND-CONSOLIDATE DNA (tier/credibility weighting) is untouched downstream."""
809	    if not rows:
810	        return [], [], []
811	
812	    from src.tools.access_bypass import detect_content_integrity_junk  # noqa: PLC0415
813	
814	    survivors: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
815	    deleted_chrome: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
816	    for row in rows:
817	        body = max(
818	            (
819	                str(row.get(k) or "") for k in (
820	                    "fetched_body", "full_text", "content", "extracted_text",
821	                    "raw_content", "raw_text", "page_text", "direct_quote",
822	                    "statement", "source_text", "body", "text",
823	                )
824	            ),
825	            key=len, default="",
826	        )
827	        url = str(row.get("source_url") or row.get("url") or "")
828	        title = str(row.get("title") or row.get("source_title") or "")
829	        try:
830	            is_junk, cls = detect_content_integrity_junk(body, url, title)
831	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-open, never delete on a predicate bug
832	            logger.warning("[outline_agent] content-integrity check errored (fail-open): %s", exc)
833	            is_junk, cls = False, ""
834	        if is_junk:
835	            copied = dict(row)
836	            copied["deletion_reason"] = f"content_integrity_junk:{cls}"
837	            deleted_chrome.append(copied)
838	        else:
839	            survivors.append(row)
840	
841	    if not survivors:
842	        return [], deleted_chrome, []
843	
844	    try:
845	        from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.topic_relevance_gate import (  # noqa: PLC0415
846	            classify_topic_relevance, topic_gate_enabled,
847	        )
848	        if not topic_gate_enabled():
849	            return survivors, deleted_chrome, []
850	        llm_callable = _sync_llm_bridge(
851	            agent_model, _env_int("PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_MAX_TOKENS", 1200),
852	        )
853	        topic_result = await asyncio.to_thread(
854	            classify_topic_relevance, survivors, research_question, llm_callable,
855	        )
856	        kept = list(getattr(topic_result, "kept_rows", None) or survivors)
857	        dropped = list(getattr(topic_result, "dropped_rows", None) or [])
858	        deleted_offtopic = []
859	        for row in dropped:
860	            copied = dict(row)
861	            copied["deletion_reason"] = "confirmed_offtopic_subject"
862	            deleted_offtopic.append(copied)
863	        return kept, deleted_chrome, deleted_offtopic
864	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — topic judge is fail-open by design
865	        logger.warning("[outline_agent] topic judge skipped (fail-open): %s", exc)
866	        return survivors, deleted_chrome, []

1895	          foreign model reference fails (P7-19).
1896	      (b) the field exists in that model's executed result.
1897	      (c) the rendered number IMMEDIATELY before the token equals the field's
1898	          canonical ``display_value`` — exact display-string match OR a tight
1899	          numeric backstop (D2). Adjacency means a modeled-assumption number
1900	          elsewhere in the sentence cannot satisfy the token (P7-20).
1901	      (d) every modeled input USED by the field is labeled "(modeled assumption)"
1902	          in the sentence (P7-7/P7-8).
1903	      (e) the field's sourced inputs all resolve in the evidence pool.
1904	
1905	    On PASS, returns SentenceVerification whose ``tokens`` are the source-input
1906	    ProvenanceTokens (so resolve_provenance_to_citations cites the inputs) — the
1907	    calc token is stripped downstream. On any failure → is_verified=False (the
1908	    whole sentence is dropped).
1909	    """
1910	    model_id = calc_match.group("model_id")
1911	    spec_hash = calc_match.group("spec_hash")
1912	    field_id = calc_match.group("field")
1913	    failures: list[str] = []
1914	
1915	    result = quantified_models.get((model_id, spec_hash))
1916	    if result is None:
1917	        return SentenceVerification(
1918	            sentence=sentence, tokens=[], is_verified=False,
1919	            failure_reasons=[f"calc_model_not_in_registry:{model_id}:{spec_hash}"],
1920	        )
1921	    fld = getattr(result, "fields", {}).get(field_id)
1922	    if not isinstance(fld, dict):
1923	        return SentenceVerification(
1924	            sentence=sentence, tokens=[], is_verified=False,
1925	            failure_reasons=[f"calc_field_unknown:{field_id}"],
1926	        )
1927	
1928	    display_value = str(fld.get("display_value", ""))
1929	
1930	    # (c) adjacency + equality (Codex diff-gate P1-1/P1-2). The rendered number
1931	    # IMMEDIATELY before the token must canonicalize to EXACTLY the field's
1932	    # display_value. Two fail-closed properties:
1933	    #   - FULL-number compare (not `before.endswith(display_value)`, which would
1934	    #     accept "123.40%" for a "23.40%" field — the wrong number ends with the
1935	    #     canonical string).
1936	    #   - RE-CANONICALIZE the parsed adjacent value through the SAME pinned
1937	    #     formatter and require an exact string match (no magnitude-scaled rel-tol
1938	    #     drift, which would accept "$1,000,000,000,999.00" for a
1939	    #     "$1,000,000,000,000.00" field). A benign reformat (missing $/commas) that
1940	    #     canonicalizes to the same string still passes; any real numeric
1941	    #     difference fails.
1942	    before = sentence[: calc_match.start()]
1943	    adj = _CALC_ADJACENT_NUMBER_RE.search(before)
1944	    if adj is None:
1945	        failures.append("calc_no_adjacent_number")
1946	    else:
1947	        adj_str = adj.group(1).strip()
1948	        ok = adj_str == display_value
1949	        if not ok:
1950	            adj_val = _calc_parse_number(adj_str)
1951	            if adj_val is not None:
1952	                from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.tradeoff_modeler import (
1953	                    _canonical_display,
1954	                )

1956	                    adj_val,
1957	                    str(fld.get("unit", "")),
1958	                    str(fld.get("display_kind", "number")),
1959	                )
1960	                ok = recanon == display_value
1961	        if not ok:
1962	            failures.append(f"calc_number_mismatch:{adj_str}!={display_value}")
1963	
1964	    # (d) modeled-assumption labeling
1965	    modeled_used = fld.get("modeled_used") or []
1966	    if modeled_used and _MODELED_ASSUMPTION_LABEL not in sentence:
1967	        failures.append(
1968	            "calc_modeled_assumption_unlabeled:" + ",".join(map(str, modeled_used))
1969	        )
1970	
1971	    # (e) sourced-input evidence resolution + ProvenanceToken construction
1972	    src_tokens: list[ProvenanceToken] = []
1973	    for t in (fld.get("sourced_tokens") or []):
1974	        ev_id = str(t.get("ev_id", ""))

547	def _locate_unique_literal(
548	    text: str, target_value: float
549	) -> tuple[str, int, int] | None:
550	    """Find the UNIQUE numeric literal in ``text`` whose extractor-normalized
551	    value matches ``target_value``. Returns (literal, start, end) or None when
552	    zero or >=2 candidates match (fail-closed disambiguation)."""
553	    if not text:
554	        return None
555	    matches: list[tuple[str, int, int]] = []
556	    for m in _LITERAL_RE.finditer(text):
557	        raw = m.group(0).strip()
558	        # Skip bare scale/percent fragments with no digit.
559	        if not re.search(r"\d", raw):
560	            continue
561	        norm = _normalize_literal(raw)
562	        if norm is None:
563	            continue
564	        if math.isclose(
565	            norm, float(target_value),
566	            rel_tol=_LITERAL_MATCH_REL_TOL, abs_tol=_LITERAL_MATCH_ABS_TOL,
567	        ):
568	            # Trim leading/trailing whitespace captured by the regex.
569	            lit = m.group(0)
570	            lstrip = len(lit) - len(lit.lstrip())
571	            rstrip = len(lit) - len(lit.rstrip())
572	            start = m.start() + lstrip
573	            end = m.end() - rstrip
574	            matches.append((text[start:end], start, end))
575	    if len(matches) == 1:
576	        return matches[0]
577	    return None

"""The verified-compute RENDER lane for the agentic outliner (W3-render, 2026-07-11).

This module closes the render half of the compute-reachability P1: it is the ONLY path by
which a number the agent DERIVED (a delta / ratio / sum) may reach rendered outline text. It
does so without ever widening the faithfulness surface, because it renders that number ONLY as
a ``[#calc:model_id:spec_hash:field]`` token — which ``strict_verify`` force-routes to
``verify_modeled_atom`` (provenance_generator.py:1885), NOT to the ``[#ev:...]`` / ``[CITE:...]``
evidence-span path. A derived number can never satisfy the evidence-span path (its digits appear
in no single source span → ``number_not_in_any_cited_span``), so it is structurally impossible to
launder an unverified computed value through a citation. Proven end-to-end in
tests/polaris_graph/outline/test_verified_compute_render.py.

The moat vs. frontier products: the number is not asserted, it is COMPUTED and PROVED. The tool
supplies {question, sourced inputs (ev_id + raw_literal via a datapoint match), formula intent};
``build_quantified_spec`` (tradeoff_modeler.py:782) validates fail-closed —
``literal_span_is_faithful``, the pure-arithmetic AST whitelist, exact-one datapoint match, and
the material-dependency perturbation gate — then ``execute_quantified_model``
(quantified_analysis.py:650) RE-DERIVES the number by executing the rendered (non-LLM) script in
the sandbox and pins its canonical ``display_value``. Regime-C verification later re-checks the
rendered digits against that re-execution.

SPEND-FREE: no network, no codegen LLM. The spec is supplied by the caller and validated
deterministically; a bad spec returns ``None`` (the whole compute is skipped, fail-closed).
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import logging
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Any, Optional

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ComputedClaim:
    """A verified, render-eligible computed number.

    ``calc_token`` is the ONLY thing that may be emitted into outline text next to
    ``display_value``; ``strict_verify`` (given the workspace's ``quantified_models`` registry)
    keeps the sentence iff the number immediately before the token canonicalizes to exactly
    ``display_value`` and every sourced input resolves. Anything else about this claim is
    telemetry.
    """

    model_id: str
    spec_hash: str
    field_id: str
    display_value: str
    calc_token: str
    question: str
    formula: str

    def render_sentence(self, lead: str) -> str:
        """A minimal, verifier-passing sentence: ``<lead> <display_value> <calc_token>.``

        ``lead`` is caller prose (e.g. "Adobe's operating income rose by"); the display value
        is placed IMMEDIATELY before the calc token (token adjacency, brief §1.4) so
        ``verify_modeled_atom`` binds the token to exactly this rendered number.
        """
        lead = lead.rstrip()
        return f"{lead} {self.display_value} {self.calc_token}."


async def run_verified_compute(
    workspace: Any,
    *,
    question: str,
    datapoints: list[dict[str, Any]],
    raw_spec: dict[str, Any],
    render_field: Optional[str] = None,
    timeout: int | None = None,
    on_reject: Optional[Any] = None,
) -> Optional[ComputedClaim]:
    """Build → validate → execute → REGISTER a verified quantified model; return a
    ``ComputedClaim`` for ONE output field (``render_field``, default: the first output).

    Fail-closed: returns ``None`` (and discloses the reject reason on the workspace) on ANY
    validation or execution failure. On success, the ``QuantifiedResult`` is registered in
    ``workspace.quantified_models`` keyed by ``(model_id, spec_hash)`` — exactly the shape
    ``strict_verify(..., quantified_models=<registry>)`` consumes — so the returned claim's
    ``calc_token`` will verify when it is later rendered into outline text.

    Parameters mirror the ``build_quantified_spec`` contract:
      * ``datapoints`` — the candidate sourced numbers (cp-format: evidence_id/label/context/
        value/unit); each referenced input matches EXACTLY one of these.
      * ``raw_spec`` — {model_id, title, inputs:[{name, datapoint_ref|modeled...}], outputs:
        [{name, formula, unit, display_kind}]}. The agent supplies the FORMULA INTENT here; the
        span derivation and re-execution are the tool's, not the agent's.
    """
    # Lazy imports: this module is loaded at agent import time; keep the heavy synthesis /
    # generator graph off the import path until a compute is actually requested.
    from src.polaris_graph.generator.quantified_analysis import execute_quantified_model  # noqa: PLC0415
    from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.tradeoff_modeler import build_quantified_spec  # noqa: PLC0415

    ev_store = getattr(workspace, "ev_store", {}) or {}

    reject_reasons: list[str] = []

    def _reject_sink(reason: str) -> None:
        reject_reasons.append(reason)
        if on_reject is not None:
            try:
                on_reject(reason)
            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — a telemetry sink must never break the compute
                pass

    def _spec_llm(_q: str, _s: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> dict[str, Any]:
        # Deterministic: the caller already produced the validated-intent spec. build_quantified_spec
        # still runs EVERY fail-closed gate over it (no LLM, no codegen, spend-free).
        return raw_spec

    try:
        spec = build_quantified_spec(
            question, datapoints, ev_store,
            spec_llm=_spec_llm, on_reject=_reject_sink,
        )
    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-closed: a builder defect skips the compute
        _disclose(workspace, f"verified_compute: spec build raised ({str(exc)[:120]}) — no number rendered")
        return None

    if spec is None:
        _disclose(
            workspace,
            f"verified_compute: spec REJECTED (fail-closed, no number rendered) "
            f"reasons={reject_reasons or ['unknown']}",
        )
        return None

    try:
        result = await execute_quantified_model(spec, ev_store, timeout=timeout)
    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — sandbox/exec failure is a clean skip
        _disclose(workspace, f"verified_compute: execution raised ({str(exc)[:120]}) — no number rendered")
        return None

    if result is None:
        _disclose(workspace, "verified_compute: model did not execute cleanly — no number rendered (fail-closed)")
        return None

    # Choose the field to render. Default: the first declared output name (its field_id == name).
    field_id = render_field or (spec.outputs[0].name if spec.outputs else "")
    disp = result.display_value(field_id)
    if not field_id or disp is None:
        _disclose(
            workspace,
            f"verified_compute: field {field_id!r} produced no display_value — no number rendered",
        )
        return None

    # REGISTER: this is what makes the calc token verifiable downstream. Keyed identically to
    # strict_verify's consumption ((model_id, spec_hash)).
    registry = _ensure_registry(workspace)
    registry[result.key()] = result

    token = result.calc_token(field_id)
    formula = ""
    out = spec.output_by_name(field_id.split("@", 1)[0])
    if out is not None:
        formula = out.formula

    _disclose(
        workspace,
        f"verified_compute: RENDER-ELIGIBLE {disp} via {token} "
        f"(formula={formula!r}, inputs={[s.name for s in spec.sourced_inputs]}) — "
        "renders ONLY through the verified [#calc:] lane, never [#ev:]/[CITE:]",
    )

    return ComputedClaim(
        model_id=result.model_id,
        spec_hash=result.spec_hash,
        field_id=field_id,
        display_value=disp,
        calc_token=token,
        question=question,
        formula=formula,
    )


def _ensure_registry(workspace: Any) -> dict:
    reg = getattr(workspace, "quantified_models", None)
    if not isinstance(reg, dict):
        reg = {}
        try:
            workspace.quantified_models = reg
        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — a frozen/odd workspace still gets a usable local registry
            pass
    return reg


def _disclose(workspace: Any, text: str) -> None:
    fn = getattr(workspace, "disclose", None)
    if callable(fn):
        fn(text)
    else:
        logger.info("[verified_compute] %s", text)

"""W3-render: the compute-reachability P1, render half — CLOSED and PROVED end-to-end.

This is the load-bearing faithfulness proof for the agentic outliner's moat (COMPUTE-and-PROVE
numbers). It exercises the REAL machinery — no mocks of the verifier, spec builder, sandbox, or
canonical formatter — on ONE real FinanceBench case (Adobe operating income change
FY2015->FY2016, gold delta ≈ $590 million).

Two invariants, proven together:
  (A) RENDER LANE: a number the agent DERIVED ($590,507,000.00 = (1,493,602 - 903,095) * 1000
      thousands USD) renders through ``strict_verify`` via its ``[#calc:...]`` token —
      kept=1, dropped=0 — and the exact canonical display string survives.
  (D) DROP GUARD: the SAME derived number, routed instead through the ``[#ev:...]`` / ``[CITE:...]``
      evidence-span path, is DROPPED (``number_not_in_any_cited_span``) — because a derived value
      appears in no single source span. This is the faithfulness invariant: a derived/unverified
      number can NEVER reach the citation render path (a P0 breach if it could).

REAL DATA: the evidence spans carry the actual 10-K operating-income literals; the span is
DERIVED by the tool (``literal_span_is_faithful``), not asserted by the test.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio

from src.polaris_graph.generator.provenance_generator import strict_verify
from src.polaris_graph.outline.outline_agent import OutlineWorkspace
from src.polaris_graph.outline.verified_compute import ComputedClaim, run_verified_compute

# Adobe 10-K, thousands USD: FY2016 operating income = 1,493,602 ; FY2015 = 903,095.
# delta = 590,507 (thousands) = $590,507,000  (FinanceBench gold ≈ $590 million).
_EV = {
    "ev_2016": {
        "evidence_id": "ev_2016",
        "direct_quote": "Adobe reported operating income of 1,493,602 for fiscal 2016.",
    },
    "ev_2015": {
        "evidence_id": "ev_2015",
        "direct_quote": "Adobe reported operating income of 903,095 for fiscal 2015.",
    },
}
_DATAPOINTS = [
    {"evidence_id": "ev_2016", "label": "opinc_2016", "context": "fiscal 2016",
     "value": "1493602", "unit": "kUSD"},
    {"evidence_id": "ev_2015", "label": "opinc_2015", "context": "fiscal 2015",
     "value": "903095", "unit": "kUSD"},
]
_RAW_SPEC = {
    "model_id": "opinc_delta",
    "title": "Adobe operating income change FY2015->FY2016",
    "inputs": [
        {"name": "opinc_2016", "datapoint_ref": {
            "ev_id": "ev_2016", "label": "opinc_2016", "context": "fiscal 2016",
            "value": "1493602", "unit": "kUSD"}},
        {"name": "opinc_2015", "datapoint_ref": {
            "ev_id": "ev_2015", "label": "opinc_2015", "context": "fiscal 2015",
            "value": "903095", "unit": "kUSD"}},
    ],
    # *1000 converts the thousands-denominated literals to dollars; the scale is a formula
    # constant (disclosed), the INPUT literals stay verbatim-cited.
    "outputs": [
        {"name": "delta", "formula": "(opinc_2016 - opinc_2015) * 1000",
         "unit": "USD", "display_kind": "currency"},
    ],
}
_QUESTION = "What was Adobe's operating income change from FY2015 to FY2016?"
_GOLD_DISPLAY = "$590,507,000.00"


def _workspace() -> OutlineWorkspace:
    return OutlineWorkspace(research_question=_QUESTION, ev_store=dict(_EV))


def _compute(ws: OutlineWorkspace) -> ComputedClaim:
    claim = asyncio.run(run_verified_compute(
        ws, question=_QUESTION, datapoints=_DATAPOINTS, raw_spec=_RAW_SPEC,
    ))
    assert claim is not None, "verified_compute returned None — the Adobe spec must build+execute"
    return claim


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- build/register


def test_verified_compute_produces_gold_display_and_registers_model():
    ws = _workspace()
    claim = _compute(ws)

    assert claim.display_value == _GOLD_DISPLAY, (
        f"gold delta must be {_GOLD_DISPLAY}, got {claim.display_value}"
    )
    assert claim.model_id == "opinc_delta"
    assert claim.field_id == "delta"
    assert claim.calc_token == f"[#calc:opinc_delta:{claim.spec_hash}:delta]"
    # The verified model is registered EXACTLY as strict_verify consumes it.
    assert (claim.model_id, claim.spec_hash) in ws.quantified_models
    # ...and this is a SEPARATE surface from exploratory execute_python output.
    assert ws.compute_results == []


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- (A) render lane


def test_derived_number_RENDERS_through_the_calc_lane_kept1_dropped0():
    ws = _workspace()
    claim = _compute(ws)

    sentence = claim.render_sentence("Adobe's operating income rose by")
    assert _GOLD_DISPLAY in sentence and claim.calc_token in sentence

    report = strict_verify(sentence, ws.ev_store, quantified_models=ws.quantified_models)

    assert report.total_kept == 1 and report.total_dropped == 0, (
        f"CALC LANE must keep the computed sentence, got "
        f"kept={report.total_kept} dropped={report.total_dropped} "
        f"reasons={[d.failure_reasons for d in report.dropped_sentences]}"
    )
    kept = report.kept_sentences[0]
    # The kept sentence's provenance tokens are the SOURCE INPUTS (the calc token strips to the
    # inputs it was derived from), so the rendered number is traceable to the two 10-K spans.
    cited = {t.evidence_id for t in kept.tokens}
    assert cited == {"ev_2016", "ev_2015"}, f"computed number must cite BOTH inputs, got {cited}"


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- (D) drop guard


def test_derived_number_via_CITE_evidence_span_is_DROPPED():
    """The faithfulness invariant: a derived number can NEVER render through [#ev:]/[CITE:].

    Route the SAME $590,507,000.00 through an evidence-span citation over a VALID in-bounds span
    of a real source. It is dropped because the derived digits appear in no single source span.
    """
    ws = _workspace()
    claim = _compute(ws)

    span_len = len(_EV["ev_2016"]["direct_quote"])
    bad = f"Adobe's operating income rose by {claim.display_value} [#ev:ev_2016:0-{span_len}]."

    report = strict_verify(bad, ws.ev_store, quantified_models=ws.quantified_models)

    assert report.total_kept == 0 and report.total_dropped == 1, (
        f"a derived number cited via an evidence span MUST be dropped, got "
        f"kept={report.total_kept} dropped={report.total_dropped}"
    )
    reasons = report.dropped_sentences[0].failure_reasons
    assert any("number_not_in_any_cited_span" in r for r in reasons), (
        f"drop must be the number-not-in-span faithfulness guard, got {reasons}"
    )


def test_a_wrong_number_next_to_the_calc_token_is_DROPPED():
    """Regime-C adjacency (check c): the calc token cannot be reused to bless a DIFFERENT number.

    If the outline text places a wrong figure immediately before the (valid, registered) calc
    token, strict_verify drops it — the token proves ONLY its own canonical display value.
    """
    ws = _workspace()
    claim = _compute(ws)

    wrong = f"Adobe's operating income rose by $999,999,999.00 {claim.calc_token}."
    report = strict_verify(wrong, ws.ev_store, quantified_models=ws.quantified_models)

    assert report.total_kept == 0 and report.total_dropped == 1, (
        "a wrong number adjacent to a real calc token must be dropped"
    )
    reasons = report.dropped_sentences[0].failure_reasons
    assert any("calc_number_mismatch" in r for r in reasons), reasons


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- fail-closed build


def test_unfaithful_literal_span_fails_closed_and_renders_nothing():
    """If the claimed input value is NOT present in the cited evidence, no model is built and no
    number becomes render-eligible (fail-closed). The registry stays empty."""
    ws = _workspace()
    bad_dps = [
        # value 9999999 is nowhere in ev_2016's text -> no unique literal span.
        {"evidence_id": "ev_2016", "label": "opinc_2016", "context": "fiscal 2016",
         "value": "9999999", "unit": "kUSD"},
        _DATAPOINTS[1],
    ]
    bad_spec = dict(_RAW_SPEC)
    bad_spec["inputs"] = [
        {"name": "opinc_2016", "datapoint_ref": {
            "ev_id": "ev_2016", "label": "opinc_2016", "context": "fiscal 2016",
            "value": "9999999", "unit": "kUSD"}},
        _RAW_SPEC["inputs"][1],
    ]

    claim = asyncio.run(run_verified_compute(
        ws, question=_QUESTION, datapoints=bad_dps, raw_spec=bad_spec,
    ))
    assert claim is None, "an unfaithful input must fail-closed (no render-eligible number)"
    assert ws.quantified_models == {}, "no model may be registered on a fail-closed build"
    assert any("REJECTED" in d for d in ws.disclosures), "the reject must be disclosed"

input.content:
"""THE shared fold-in seam for the agentic outliner (factored 2026-07-11).

Every path that brings EXTERNAL fetched content into ``workspace.ev_store`` — ``search_more_evidence``
today, ``fetch_url`` next — MUST re-enter through this ONE seam so the faithfulness engine stays the
only hard gate and the id space can never collide. Factored out of ``outline_agent.py`` (was
``_offset_renumber`` :629 + ``_stamp_and_delete`` :802) so both tools share the identical unit
instead of copy-pasting it.

The seam, in order (design §):
  1. URL-dedup vs. the rows already in ``ev_store`` (never re-fold a page we already hold).
  2. ``_offset_renumber`` — assign ``ev_{offset+i}`` ids and HARD fail-loud if any new id collides
     with an existing id (a collision is a programming bug in the offset math, not a data
     condition — LAW II: never swallow).
  3. ``_stamp_and_delete`` — S2 stamp pass: content-integrity chrome delete (pure) + topic-judge
     off-topic delete (LLM, fail-open). §-1.3.1 junk carve-out only; the rest of
     WEIGHT-AND-CONSOLIDATE is untouched downstream.
  4. Insert the survivors into ``ev_store``.

No number and no citation is ever invented here; this seam only renumbers, dedups, and deletes
junk. Computed numbers take the SEPARATE verified ``[#calc:]`` lane (outline/verified_compute.py).
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import logging
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Any

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


def _offset_renumber(
    new_rows: list[dict[str, Any]], offset: int, existing_ids: set[str],
) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    """THE sharp seam (design §): renumber ``new_rows`` to ``ev_{offset+i}`` and HARD
    fail-loud assert the new id set never intersects ``existing_ids``. A collision here is a
    programming bug in the offset computation, not a data condition — it must never be
    swallowed (LAW II: fail loudly, never silently degrade)."""
    renumbered: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    new_ids: set[str] = set()
    for i, row in enumerate(new_rows):
        new_id = f"ev_{offset + i:03d}"
        if new_id in existing_ids or new_id in new_ids:
            raise AssertionError(
                f"outline_agent id-collision seam FAILED: {new_id!r} already present "
                f"(existing_ids has it: {new_id in existing_ids}; batch dup: {new_id in new_ids}). "
                "This must never happen — the offset computation has a bug."
            )
        new_ids.add(new_id)
        copied = dict(row)
        copied["evidence_id"] = new_id
        renumbered.append(copied)
    assert new_ids.isdisjoint(existing_ids), (
        "outline_agent id-collision seam FAILED post-hoc: new ids intersect existing ids "
        f"({sorted(new_ids & existing_ids)})"
    )
    return renumbered


async def _stamp_and_delete(
    rows: list[dict[str, Any]], research_question: str, agent_model: str,
) -> tuple[list[dict[str, Any]], list[dict[str, Any]], list[dict[str, Any]]]:
    """S2 stamp pass: content-integrity chrome detection (pure, no LLM) THEN semantic
    topic-judge off-topic screen (LLM, fail-open). Returns (kept, deleted_chrome,
    deleted_offtopic). §-1.3.1: only genuine junk/off-topic is deleted here — the rest of
    the WEIGHT-AND-CONSOLIDATE DNA (tier/credibility weighting) is untouched downstream."""
    if not rows:
        return [], [], []

    from src.tools.access_bypass import detect_content_integrity_junk  # noqa: PLC0415

    survivors: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    deleted_chrome: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    for row in rows:
        body = max(
            (
                str(row.get(k) or "") for k in (
                    "fetched_body", "full_text", "content", "extracted_text",
                    "raw_content", "raw_text", "page_text", "direct_quote",
                    "statement", "source_text", "body", "text",
                )
            ),
            key=len, default="",
        )
        url = str(row.get("source_url") or row.get("url") or "")
        title = str(row.get("title") or row.get("source_title") or "")
        try:
            is_junk, cls = detect_content_integrity_junk(body, url, title)
        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-open, never delete on a predicate bug
            logger.warning("[outline_agent] content-integrity check errored (fail-open): %s", exc)
            is_junk, cls = False, ""
        if is_junk:
            copied = dict(row)
            copied["deletion_reason"] = f"content_integrity_junk:{cls}"
            deleted_chrome.append(copied)
        else:
            survivors.append(row)

    if not survivors:
        return [], deleted_chrome, []

    try:
        # Lazy imports from outline_agent avoid a module-load import cycle (outline_agent imports
        # THIS module at top). By call time outline_agent is fully initialized.
        from src.polaris_graph.outline.outline_agent import (  # noqa: PLC0415
            _env_int, _sync_llm_bridge,
        )
        from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.topic_relevance_gate import (  # noqa: PLC0415
            classify_topic_relevance, topic_gate_enabled,
        )
        if not topic_gate_enabled():
            return survivors, deleted_chrome, []
        llm_callable = _sync_llm_bridge(
            agent_model, _env_int("PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_MAX_TOKENS", 1200),
        )
        import asyncio  # noqa: PLC0415
        topic_result = await asyncio.to_thread(
            classify_topic_relevance, survivors, research_question, llm_callable,
        )
        kept = list(getattr(topic_result, "kept_rows", None) or survivors)
        dropped = list(getattr(topic_result, "dropped_rows", None) or [])
        deleted_offtopic = []
        for row in dropped:
            copied = dict(row)
            copied["deletion_reason"] = "confirmed_offtopic_subject"
            deleted_offtopic.append(copied)
        return kept, deleted_chrome, deleted_offtopic
    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — topic judge is fail-open by design
        logger.warning("[outline_agent] topic judge skipped (fail-open): %s", exc)
        return survivors, deleted_chrome, []


@dataclass
class FoldInResult:
    """The outcome of one fold-in of external fetched rows through the shared seam."""

    kept_rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=list)
    n_url_dup: int = 0
    deleted_chrome: list[dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=list)
    deleted_offtopic: list[dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=list)

    @property
    def n_kept(self) -> int:
        return len(self.kept_rows)

    @property
    def n_deleted(self) -> int:
        return len(self.deleted_chrome) + len(self.deleted_offtopic)


async def fold_in_fetched_rows(
    workspace: Any,
    fetched_rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
    *,
    research_question: str,
    agent_model: str,
    insert: bool = True,
) -> FoldInResult:
    """The reusable fold-in unit: url-dedup -> offset-renumber (hard id-collision assert) ->
    S2 stamp/delete -> insert survivors into ``workspace.ev_store``.

    Shared by ``search_more_evidence`` and (next) ``fetch_url`` so a second content path can NEVER
    diverge from the id-collision guard or the junk/off-topic screen. Returns a ``FoldInResult``
    carrying the survivors and per-stage counts for the caller's disclosure/markdown. ``insert``
    is always True in production; a caller may set it False to inspect the survivors without
    mutating the store.
    """
    existing_urls = workspace.existing_urls()
    deduped_rows = [
        r for r in fetched_rows
        if str(r.get("source_url") or r.get("url") or "").strip() not in existing_urls
        or not str(r.get("source_url") or r.get("url") or "").strip()
    ]
    n_url_dup = len(fetched_rows) - len(deduped_rows)

    offset = workspace.next_ev_offset()
    existing_ids = set(workspace.ev_store.keys())
    renumbered = _offset_renumber(deduped_rows, offset, existing_ids)

    kept_rows, deleted_chrome, deleted_offtopic = await _stamp_and_delete(
        renumbered, research_question, agent_model,
    )

    if insert:
        for row in kept_rows:
            workspace.ev_store[row["evidence_id"]] = row

    return FoldInResult(
        kept_rows=kept_rows,
        n_url_dup=n_url_dup,
        deleted_chrome=deleted_chrome,
        deleted_offtopic=deleted_offtopic,
    )

"""The shared fold-in seam (outline/_fold_in.py) — the ONE unit both search_more_evidence and
fetch_url re-enter external content through. Locks the id-collision fail-loud guard and the
end-to-end url-dedup -> renumber -> stamp -> insert orchestrator.

Faithfulness-critical: a second content path (fetch_url) that diverged from this seam could
re-use an existing ev_id and silently overwrite a real evidence row (LAW II breach). The
id-collision assert here is the guard that makes that structurally impossible.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio

import pytest

from src.polaris_graph.outline._fold_in import (
    FoldInResult,
    _offset_renumber,
    fold_in_fetched_rows,
)
from src.polaris_graph.outline.outline_agent import OutlineWorkspace


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- _offset_renumber


def test_offset_renumber_assigns_sequential_ids_from_offset():
    rows = [{"source_url": "http://a", "direct_quote": "x"},
            {"source_url": "http://b", "direct_quote": "y"}]
    out = _offset_renumber(rows, offset=5, existing_ids={"ev_000", "ev_004"})
    assert [r["evidence_id"] for r in out] == ["ev_005", "ev_006"]
    # originals untouched (copied)
    assert "evidence_id" not in rows[0]


def test_offset_renumber_fail_loud_on_collision_with_existing():
    """A new id landing on an existing id is a programming bug in the offset math — it must
    RAISE, never silently overwrite a real evidence row."""
    with pytest.raises(AssertionError, match="id-collision seam FAILED"):
        _offset_renumber([{"x": 1}], offset=3, existing_ids={"ev_003"})


def test_offset_renumber_empty_is_noop():
    assert _offset_renumber([], offset=0, existing_ids=set()) == []


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- fold_in orchestrator


def _ws(existing: dict | None = None) -> OutlineWorkspace:
    return OutlineWorkspace(research_question="does X work?", ev_store=dict(existing or {}))


def _run(coro):
    return asyncio.run(coro)


def test_fold_in_dedups_by_url_renumbers_and_inserts(monkeypatch):
    # Neutralize the S2 stamp pass so this test isolates dedup/renumber/insert (the stamp pass
    # has its own fail-open contract; here we assert the seam plumbing).
    import src.polaris_graph.outline._fold_in as fi

    async def _passthrough(rows, rq, model):
        return list(rows), [], []

    monkeypatch.setattr(fi, "_stamp_and_delete", _passthrough)

    ws = _ws({"ev_000": {"evidence_id": "ev_000", "source_url": "http://known"}})
    fetched = [
        {"source_url": "http://known", "direct_quote": "dup — already held"},   # url-dup, dropped
        {"source_url": "http://new1", "direct_quote": "fresh one"},
        {"source_url": "http://new2", "direct_quote": "fresh two"},
    ]
    res: FoldInResult = _run(fold_in_fetched_rows(
        ws, fetched, research_question=ws.research_question, agent_model="stub/agent",
    ))

    assert res.n_url_dup == 1
    assert res.n_kept == 2
    # renumbered from next_ev_offset()==1, disjoint from the existing ev_000
    assert [r["evidence_id"] for r in res.kept_rows] == ["ev_001", "ev_002"]
    # inserted into the store, existing row untouched
    assert set(ws.ev_store) == {"ev_000", "ev_001", "ev_002"}
    assert ws.ev_store["ev_000"]["source_url"] == "http://known"


def test_fold_in_never_collides_across_two_sequential_folds(monkeypatch):
    """search_more_evidence then fetch_url (two folds) must produce a disjoint id space — the
    second fold's offset is computed from the store the first mutated."""
    import src.polaris_graph.outline._fold_in as fi

    async def _passthrough(rows, rq, model):
        return list(rows), [], []

    monkeypatch.setattr(fi, "_stamp_and_delete", _passthrough)

    ws = _ws()
    first = _run(fold_in_fetched_rows(
        ws, [{"source_url": "http://a", "direct_quote": "a"}],
        research_question=ws.research_question, agent_model="stub",
    ))
    second = _run(fold_in_fetched_rows(
        ws, [{"source_url": "http://b", "direct_quote": "b"}],
        research_question=ws.research_question, agent_model="stub",
    ))
    ids = [r["evidence_id"] for r in first.kept_rows + second.kept_rows]
    assert ids == ["ev_000", "ev_001"], ids
    assert len(set(ws.ev_store)) == 2, "no id-collision across folds"


def test_fold_in_insert_false_leaves_store_untouched(monkeypatch):
    import src.polaris_graph.outline._fold_in as fi

    async def _passthrough(rows, rq, model):
        return list(rows), [], []

    monkeypatch.setattr(fi, "_stamp_and_delete", _passthrough)

    ws = _ws()
    res = _run(fold_in_fetched_rows(
        ws, [{"source_url": "http://a", "direct_quote": "a"}],
        research_question=ws.research_question, agent_model="stub", insert=False,
    ))
    assert res.n_kept == 1
    assert ws.ev_store == {}, "insert=False must not mutate the store"

111	    basket_lines: list[str]                 # one line per multi-member finding cluster
112	    singleton_lines: list[str]              # one line per row in no multi-member cluster
113	    ev_id_to_basket: dict[str, str]         # member ev_id -> basket id (full map; never elided)
114	    total_chars: int                        # rendered menu size (headroom accounting)
115	    degraded: bool = False                  # a headroom-guard terse pass fired
116	    # item 11: True when EVEN the fully-tersed+elided menu is STILL over ``max_chars`` — a
117	    # pathological pool that would ship a silently oversized prompt. Disclosed (never silent): the
118	    # builder also logs a WARNING, and the caller must surface/act on this flag (fail loud).
119	    oversized: bool = False
120	    basket_member_ev_ids: dict[str, list[str]] = field(default_factory=dict)
121	    # PUSH A: canonical singleton ev_id -> its FOLDED same-work alias ev_ids (the copies of the
122	    # same underlying work that collapsed onto that one singleton line). Empty when
123	    # ``same_work_groups`` was not supplied. ``covered_ev_ids()`` unions these so every alias is
124	    # still accounted for by the 100%-of-pool invariant (§-1.3 consolidate — folded, never dropped).
125	    singleton_alias_ev_ids: dict[str, list[str]] = field(default_factory=dict)
126	    # PUSH A: basket id -> WORK-level corroboration (distinct works among the basket's members,
127	    # deduped through the same-work alias map). Equals the row count when no members share a work
128	    # (so it is byte-identical to ``len(members)`` when ``same_work_groups`` is absent). This is the
129	    # honest corroboration the orphan check reads — 4 rows of 2 works corroborate a claim TWICE.
130	    basket_work_corroboration: dict[str, int] = field(default_factory=dict)
